name: eBay REST APIs
what_it_does: Provides REST APIs for managing eBay listings, processing orders, tracking inventory, and handling seller account operations on the eBay marketplace.
use_cases: Automated listing creation and price adjustment for seller inventory
Order fulfillment pipeline: pull new orders, update shipment tracking, close transactions
Inventory synchronization between a warehouse system and eBay active listings
Competitor price monitoring using Browse API to search live listings
Account health monitoring and policy compliance reporting for seller dashboards
not_for: Building a consumer-facing eBay shopping app (use Browse API only, heavily restricted)
High-frequency trading or real-time auction bidding automation (against eBay policy)
Accessing buyer personal data beyond what is required for order fulfillment
best_when: Best when building seller-side automation tools that need to manage listings, process orders, and sync inventory across multiple channels.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need real-time auction bid management or when your use case involves scraping competitor data at scale, as both violate eBay policy.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with two token types: Application tokens (Client Credentials grant, user-independent, for public data) and User tokens (Authorization Code grant, for seller operations). Sandbox and Production environments require separate credentials. Token expiration: app tokens ~2 hours, user tokens ~18 months with refresh.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access is free; standard eBay seller fees apply per transaction. Developer accounts get sandbox access and production access at no charge.
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid API tiers; costs are eBay marketplace fees (insertion fees, final value fees) on actual sales.
pricing.notes: Developer registration is free. Production API access granted after sandbox testing. Call volume limits apply per application.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by API family and endpoint. Fulfillment API: 10,000 calls/day. Inventory API: 1,000 calls/day per default. Browse API: 5,000 calls/day. Limits can be increased via request. No per-second burst limits documented publicly.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include error IDs and messages but can be verbose and inconsistent across API families. Some errors require cross-referencing the error catalog separately.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Inventory API supports idempotent PUT operations for listings. Order operations are generally safe to retry but lack explicit idempotency keys. Create operations can result in duplicates if retried naively.
known_agent_gotchas: Two distinct token types (application vs user) must be used for the correct API family — mixing them causes cryptic 401 errors
Sandbox and production have separate app credentials and separate eBay accounts; testing in sandbox does not guarantee production behavior
Daily call limits per API family are low by default (e.g., 1,000/day for Inventory); agents must implement call budgeting or risk being blocked mid-workflow
Listing lifecycle has many states (active, ended, sold, unsold) and transitions are not always reversible — agents must check current state before mutation
API families (Browse, Fulfillment, Inventory, Account) use different base URLs and versioning schemes, requiring careful routing logic in agent tool implementations

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 is well-implemented with separate scopes per API family. Sandbox isolation is good for testing. No PKCE requirement for server-side flows. Refresh token rotation is not automatic.
